proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
sex_female is associated with a difference in pfs_months versus male patients.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import linregress; linregress(df['ecog_ps'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate slope of pfs_months on ecog_ps = -1.190 months per ECOG point (p<1e-300). Each one-point ECOG increase is associated with ~1.19 fewer months of PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Univariate slope on stage_iv = -1.352 months (p<1e-300). Stage IV patients have ~1.35 fewer months of PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Slope on sex_female = -0.006 months (p=0.77). No detectable PFS difference between sexes. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope, typical oncology prior).
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than left-sided primary tumors.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = +0.177 months per year of age (p<1e-300). Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis: older patients have LONGER PFS in this cohort. Hypothesis REFUTED in direction (significant in reverse).
Slope = -0.305 months (p=5.2e-46). Right-sided primary is associated with ~0.31 months shorter PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than KRAS wild-type.
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than BRAF wild-type.
nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than NRAS wild-type.
msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than MSS patients (better prognosis prior).

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.327 months (p=8.5e-56). KRAS-mutant patients have ~0.33 months shorter PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Slope = -0.228 months (p=3.7e-6). BRAF V600E mutants have ~0.23 months shorter PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Slope = +0.221 months (p=2.3e-4). NRAS-mutant patients have slightly LONGER PFS, opposite to expectation. Effect small. Hypothesis REFUTED in direction.
Slope = -0.019 months (p=0.68). No detectable difference between MSI-H and MSS patients. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: Slope = +0.465 months per g/dL (p=3.3e-115). Each 1 g/dL increase in albumin = ~0.47 months longer PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Slope = -0.0053 months per ng/mL (p=3.1e-17). Higher CEA -> shorter PFS. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Slope = -0.073 months per percentage point of weight loss (p=2.2e-167). Strong negative association. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Slope = -0.00032 months per U/L (p=0.013). Weak negative association at borderline significance. Hypothesis SUPPORTED in direction; small magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
her2_amplified=1 is associated with a different pfs_months than HER2-non-amplified.
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with a different pfs_months than NTRK-non-fused.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = -0.002 months per mg/L (p=0.065). Trend in expected direction but not significant at alpha=0.05. Hypothesis NOT SUPPORTED at alpha=0.05.
Slope = +0.005 months per NLR unit (p=0.29). No detectable association in univariate analysis. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Slope = -0.056 months (p=0.35). No detectable PFS difference. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Slope = +0.040 months (p=0.78). No detectable PFS difference (n=251 NTRK-fused). Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_bevacizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_encorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.
treatment_regorafenib=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than untreated.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS on cetuximab=4.285 vs off=4.323 (diff=-0.038, p=0.088). No detectable benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Mean PFS on bevacizumab=4.301 vs off=4.320 (diff=-0.019, p=0.354). No detectable benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Mean PFS on pembrolizumab=4.316 vs off=4.311 (diff=+0.005, p=0.86). No detectable benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Mean PFS on encorafenib=4.317 vs off=4.311 (diff=+0.005, p=0.87). No detectable benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Mean PFS on trastuzumab+tucatinib=4.272 vs off=4.315 (diff=-0.044, p=0.24). No detectable benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Mean PFS on regorafenib=5.089 vs off=4.117 (diff=+0.972, p=1.7e-216). LARGE main effect: ~1 month PFS gain in the marginal pool. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months specifically in patients with msi_high=1, with positive interaction (T*B coefficient > 0).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + B + T:B for pembro x msi_high; ttest within msi_high==1
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient pembrolizumab*msi_high = +0.002, p=0.99. Within MSI-high (n=2513): mean PFS pembro=4.299 vs off=4.292 (diff=+0.007, p=0.96). Hypothesis REFUTED — no benefit even in the canonical biomarker subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with braf_v600e=1, with positive interaction.
Encorafenib + cetuximab combination further extends pfs_months in BRAF V600E-mutant patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient encorafenib*braf_v600e = -0.141, p=0.38. Within BRAF V600E (n=2272): on encorafenib=3.979 vs off=4.108 (diff=-0.129, p=0.33). Hypothesis REFUTED — no benefit (numerically worse).

analyses.code: subset braf_v600e==1; combo (enc=1 & cet=1) vs other
analyses.result_summary: BRAF V600E with encorafenib+cetuximab combo (n=58) vs other (n=2214): diff=+0.058, p=0.84. No detectable combination benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with her2_amplified=1, with positive interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient trastuzumab_tucatinib*her2_amplified = +0.063, p=0.78. Within HER2-amplified (n=1504): on=4.273 vs off=4.256 (diff=+0.017, p=0.93). Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months in patients who are kras_mutation=0 AND nras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (RAS/BRAF wild-type, left-sided).

analyses.code: subset kras=0 & nras=0 & braf=0 & right=0; ttest cetuximab on/off
analyses.result_summary: RAS/BRAF wild-type left-sided subgroup (n=16443): on cetuximab=4.617 vs off=4.684 (diff=-0.067, p=0.13). Hypothesis REFUTED — cetuximab does not improve PFS even in the textbook subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by kras_mutation: regorafenib improves PFS in KRAS wild-type but loses its benefit in KRAS-mutant.
The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by braf_v600e: regorafenib improves PFS in BRAF wild-type but loses its benefit in BRAF V600E.
The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by right_sided_primary: regorafenib improves PFS in left-sided but loses its benefit in right-sided primary.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + B + T:B for regorafenib x kras
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -1.629, p=7.7e-226. Stratified: KRAS-WT regorafenib effect = +1.656 months (p<1e-292); KRAS-mutant effect = +0.027 (p=0.46). KRAS mutation completely abolishes benefit. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Interaction beta = -1.183, p=6.1e-23. Stratified: BRAF-WT effect = +1.027 (p<1e-225); BRAF V600E effect = -0.156 (p=0.13). BRAF V600E abolishes benefit. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Interaction beta = -1.454, p=4.1e-168. Stratified: left-sided effect = +1.480 (p<1e-273); right-sided effect = +0.026 (p=0.52). Right-sided primary abolishes benefit. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib improves pfs_months substantially and ONLY in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 (KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided); outside this triple-positive subgroup it has no effect.

analyses.code: stratify on kras x braf x right; ttest regorafenib in/out
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (kras=0 & braf=0 & right=0, n=17,372): regorafenib raises PFS by +2.758 months (p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=32,628): diff=+0.030, p=0.29. Effect entirely concentrated in this subgroup. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is further modified by cea_ng_ml: lower CEA enhances and higher CEA attenuates the benefit (negative T:CEA interaction).
The regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by ecog_ps (effect smaller in higher-ECOG patients).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + CEA + T:CEA, full sample then within k0/b0/r0
analyses.result_summary: Full sample T:CEA beta = -0.0253, p=8.7e-58. Within KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup: beta = -0.065, p=7.4e-136. Higher CEA attenuates regorafenib benefit. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: stratify by ecog_ps; ttest regorafenib on/off
analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib effect by ECOG: ecog=0 diff=+0.925 (p=5e-76), ecog=1 diff=+0.995 (p=4e-122), ecog=2 diff=+0.896 (p=3e-34). Effect essentially independent of ECOG. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, the regorafenib benefit on pfs_months is concentrated at low CEA (cea_ng_ml < ~5 ng/mL) and disappears above ~5 ng/mL.

analyses.code: within KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided: scan CEA thresholds 3..10 ng/mL
analyses.result_summary: Threshold scan: CEA<3: low diff=+5.03, high diff=+1.33; CEA<4: low=+5.04, high=+0.69; CEA<5: low=+5.01 (n=9326), high=+0.005 (p=0.93, n=8046); CEA<6: low=+4.55, high=-0.018 (p=0.77); CEA<8: low=+3.90, high=-0.005. Sharp threshold at ~5 ng/mL: below it regorafenib roughly doubles PFS, above it has no effect. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months ONLY in patients who simultaneously satisfy kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this fourfold subgroup the regorafenib effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=9326): mean PFS regorafenib=9.101 vs off=4.088 (diff=+5.013, p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=40,674): on=4.147 vs off=4.117 (diff=+0.025, p=0.33). The regorafenib treatment effect is confined to this 4-feature subgroup: KRAS-WT, BRAF-WT, left-sided primary, CEA<5 ng/mL. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided subgroup, nras_mutation status does not modify the regorafenib effect (NRAS-mutant patients still derive similar benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS-WT/BRAF-WT/left-sided: NRAS-WT (n=16,443) regorafenib diff=+2.749 (p<1e-300); NRAS-mutant (n=929) diff=+2.906 (p=7e-25). Effects indistinguishable; NRAS does NOT need to be added to the subgroup definition. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib with respect to any continuous lab feature (significant T:F interaction at p<0.001).

analyses.code: for T in {cetux,bev,pembro,enco,tras}: OLS pfs ~ T + F + T:F over 9 continuous features
analyses.result_summary: Of 5 treatments x 9 continuous features = 45 interaction tests, NONE reached p<0.01. Strongest hint was pembrolizumab*crp_mg_l (beta=-0.0068, p=0.020). Hypothesis REFUTED at study-wide alpha — no continuous-feature treatment-effect heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity exists for any non-regorafenib treatment with respect to any of the 8 binary features (kras, nras, braf, msi, her2, ntrk, right-sided, stage IV) at p<0.001.
T-learner CATE estimates from gradient-boosted regression trees show meaningful heterogeneity (CATE std > 0.5 months) for at least one non-regorafenib treatment.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + B + T:B over 5 treatments x 8 binary features = 40 tests
analyses.result_summary: No interaction reached p<0.001. The most extreme were bevacizumab*braf_v600e (beta=-0.228, p=0.022) and pembrolizumab*right_sided_primary (beta=-0.116, p=0.050). After Bonferroni (40 tests) nothing remains significant. Hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: T-learner with GradientBoostingRegressor for each non-regorafenib T
analyses.result_summary: CATE std: cetuximab=0.16, bevacizumab=0.15, pembrolizumab=0.19, encorafenib=0.22, trastuzumab+tucatinib=0.25. All <0.5 months. Hypothesis REFUTED — no meaningful CATE heterogeneity for non-regorafenib treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: right_sided_primary x kras_mutation interact on pfs_months (the negative effects do not simply add).
albumin_g_dl x stage_iv interact on pfs_months (low albumin more harmful in stage IV).
weight_loss_pct_6mo x ecog_ps interact on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = +0.452, p=1.7e-25. The two negative main effects are largely sub-additive: KRAS mutation adds little additional harm in already-right-sided tumors. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
Interaction beta = +0.032, p=0.41. No detectable interaction. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Interaction beta = +0.002, p=0.59. No detectable weight loss x ECOG interaction. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a full multivariable OLS controlling for all features and treatments, regorafenib remains significantly associated with longer PFS while other treatments remain non-significant.
After multivariable adjustment, age_years retains a positive coefficient on pfs_months (older = longer PFS), in contradiction to typical oncology priors.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients (n=50,000, R^2=0.86): regorafenib +0.943 (p<1e-300); cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19); bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73); pembrolizumab +0.0003 (p=0.98); encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63); trastuzumab+tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.07). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Adjusted age coefficient = +0.176 months per year (p<1e-300). Direction unchanged after full adjustment. Older patients have LONGER PFS in this dataset — contrary to typical oncology priors. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv x ecog_ps interact such that high-ECOG stage IV patients have super-additively shorter PFS.
Patients with both stage_iv=1 AND ecog_ps>=1 have mean pfs_months below 4 months, while patients with stage_iv=0 AND ecog_ps=0 have mean pfs_months above 5 months.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction S:E beta = +0.063, p=0.019. Borderline-significant in the SUB-additive direction (positive interaction means combined effect less negative than additive). Hypothesis REFUTED in direction.
Mean PFS: stage=0/ecog=0 = 6.04; stage=1/ecog>=1 = 3.19. Difference of ~2.85 months. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: best-prognosis cell exceeds 5 months and worst-prognosis cell falls well below 4 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise treatment-treatment interactions exist on pfs_months (e.g., regorafenib+bevacizumab synergy or antagonism).

analyses.code: for each pair T1,T2: OLS pfs ~ T1 + T2 + T1:T2
analyses.result_summary: All 15 pairwise treatment interaction tests had p>=0.05. Strongest was cetuximab*pembrolizumab (beta=-0.121, p=0.051). No detectable synergy or antagonism between any two treatments at study-wide alpha. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner gradient-boosted tree on the regorafenib treatment effect identifies kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary, and cea_ng_ml as the dominant treatment-effect modifiers (confirming the joint subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib CATE: mean=0.93, std=1.88. Top decision-tree feature importances on CATE: right_sided_primary=0.39, kras_mutation=0.32, cea_ng_ml=0.21, braf_v600e=0.08 (others negligible). Mean CATE inside the 4-pred subgroup = +4.83 months; outside = +0.04 months. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — same four features identified by interaction tests.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in left-sided primary tumors (left-sided MSI-H) compared to right-sided MSI-H.
Within MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab benefit might be detectable in stage IV patients specifically.

analyses.code: subset msi_high==1; ttest pembro on/off by right_sided
analyses.result_summary: MSI-H left-sided (n=1618): pembro on=4.468, off=4.358 (diff=+0.109, p=0.53). MSI-H right-sided (n=895): on=4.143, off=4.339 (diff=-0.197, p=0.29). Neither slice shows benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
MSI-H stage IV (n=1404): pembro on=4.116, off=4.188 (diff=-0.072, p=0.66). No benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL refined treatment-effect-heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months: treatment_regorafenib increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months EXCLUSIVELY in patients with kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0 AND cea_ng_ml<5; outside this 4-predicate subgroup the regorafenib effect is indistinguishable from zero.

None of treatment_cetuximab, treatment_bevacizumab, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_encorafenib, or treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib has a detectable beneficial effect on pfs_months in any subgroup defined by demographic, clinical, biomarker, or laboratory features in this cohort, even within their canonical biomarker-matched subpopulations (MSI-H, BRAF V600E, HER2-amplified, RAS/BRAF-WT left-sided).

In a final multivariable model the strongest prognostic features for pfs_months are (in descending |effect|) stage_iv (-1.36), ecog_ps (-1.17/level), albumin_g_dl (+0.47/g/dL), braf_v600e (-0.36), kras_mutation (-0.35), right_sided_primary (-0.29), age_years (+0.18/yr), weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.076/%), and cea_ng_ml (-0.005/ng/mL).

analyses.result_summary: Inside (n=9326): regorafenib mean PFS = 9.101 vs off = 4.088, diff = +5.013 months (p<1e-300). Outside (n=40,674): diff = +0.025 (p=0.33). Confirms refined subgroup is correct and complete.

Adjusted: cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab +0.0003 (p=0.98), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63), trastuzumab+tucatinib +0.026 (p=0.07). All negligible. Subgroup-targeted tests in canonical biomarker matches (MSI-H, BRAF V600E, HER2-amp, RAS/BRAF-WT left-sided) were all null. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: Listed coefficients (multivariable, R^2=0.86): stage_iv=-1.362, ecog_ps=-1.171, albumin_g_dl=+0.469, braf_v600e=-0.357, kras_mutation=-0.354, right_sided_primary=-0.287, age_years=+0.176, weight_loss_pct_6mo=-0.076, cea_ng_ml=-0.005. All p<1e-50 except age (p<1e-300, very large). Direction and magnitude confirmed. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
